# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our Python Flask API. The app needs to install dependencies from requirements.txt, expose port 5000, and run the Flask application. Keep it simple and straightforward for quick deployment to production.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official python runtime as a parent image (focal)
FROM python:3.8-slim-buster
 
# Set work directory in container   
WORKDIR /app
  
# Copy requirements file into the docker container and install dependencies using pip tool    
COPY requirements.txt ./requirements.txt        # copy your requirement files to Dockerfile's current working dir on build context (./) 
RUN pip3 install -r requirements.txt             # Install all packages listed in "requirements" file present at the above mentioned path, using python package installer tool(pip).   Requirement of a Python Flask App will be installed inside this Dockerfile as well since it is required to run an application which requires setup and configuration from dependencies
 
# Copy source code into container.    # copy your project's current directory (.) contents into the docker image at /app (/home/python_flask) on build context, so that you can use 'docker-compose up --build')    
COPY . ./                                             
  
EXPOSE 5000                                             # expose port to be used by Flask application.   
# Run app using python's flask command and run the container on Docker host at default (i.e., localhost:5000)    
CMD ["python3", "./app.py"]                                   # Execute this file as a program when running docker image in terminal or cli  ./ is for local directory, so if your app and requirements are inside the same folder then only use one command to run it by specifying path "/".    The CMD instruction sets default commands that will be executed whenever an instance of Image runs.
